_comment: Solution-pattern catalog for the AI Cost Planner. Labels, descriptions and blueprint text are user-facing and must stay jargon-free (enforced by tests/test_estimator.py). Numeric defaults are per-request shape assumptions a non-expert should not have to guess; every one can be overridden in the review screen.

shared_blueprint.title: How quality is checked
shared_blueprint.body: Every answer passes automated code-based checks (format, safety rules, response time) at near-zero cost. Depending on the tier you choose, a share of answers also gets an AI-graded review - a second AI model grades the answer against a rubric. The cost of these reviews is shown separately as the checking cost, so you always see what running the system costs versus what checking it costs.

shared_blueprint.title: How it stays reviewable
shared_blueprint.body: Every request writes an activity record, and each step the system takes is recorded separately with its inputs and outputs. That step-by-step record is what makes it possible to review not just the final answer but how the system got there - required for the compliance tier, and priced into the storage line so it is never a surprise.

shared_blueprint.title: Where it can run
shared_blueprint.body: The estimate shows the same solution priced two ways: run mostly inside Databricks (search index, model serving and working state as managed Databricks services) or mostly on Azure services (managed search, Kubernetes hosting and a managed database). Both are legitimate; the comparison shows what each choice costs at each tier.

doc_chat.label: Chat with your documents
doc_chat.description: People ask questions in plain language and get answers grounded in your own files - PDFs, wikis, policies, contracts.
doc_chat.example_prompt: What does our travel policy say about seat upgrades?

blueprint.title: What it does
blueprint.body: People type a question. The system looks up the most relevant passages from your document collection, then an AI model writes an answer grounded in those passages, with references back to the source files.

blueprint.title: What gets built
blueprint.body: A searchable index of your documents that stays in sync as files change, an AI model that writes the answers, and a small always-on service that connects the two behind your access controls.

summarize.label: Summarize long content
summarize.description: Turn long reports, transcripts or ticket threads into short, consistent summaries.
summarize.example_prompt: Give me a one-page summary of this 60-page audit report.

blueprint.title: What it does
blueprint.body: Each document or conversation is sent to an AI model with instructions describing the summary format your team needs, and the model returns the finished summary.

blueprint.title: What gets built
blueprint.body: A service that accepts content, applies your summary rules, and returns the result to the tool your team already works in.

extract_fields.label: Pull structured data out of documents
extract_fields.description: Read invoices, forms or contracts and fill a spreadsheet or system of record with the fields that matter.
extract_fields.example_prompt: From each invoice, capture vendor, amount, due date and PO number.

blueprint.title: What it does
blueprint.body: Each document is read by an AI model that fills in a fixed list of fields you define. Results land in your downstream system with a confidence note per field.

blueprint.title: What gets built
blueprint.body: A document intake step, the AI extraction service with your field definitions, and checks that flag low-confidence answers for a person to review.

classify_route.label: Sort and route incoming requests
classify_route.description: Automatically label incoming emails, tickets or claims and send each one to the right queue or team.
classify_route.example_prompt: Tag each support ticket by product area and urgency, then route it.

blueprint.title: What it does
blueprint.body: Every incoming item is labeled by an AI model against your category list and routed by rules you control. Unclear cases go to a person instead of guessing.

blueprint.title: What gets built
blueprint.body: A labeling service wired into your intake channel, the routing rules, and a review queue for low-confidence items.

agent_workflow.label: Automate a multi-step task
agent_workflow.description: An AI assistant that works through a task in several steps - looking things up, drafting, checking - before returning a finished result.
agent_workflow.example_prompt: Research this account, draft a renewal proposal, and check it against our pricing rules.

blueprint.title: What it does
blueprint.body: The assistant breaks the task into steps, uses your data sources and tools at each step, and assembles a finished result. Every intermediate step is recorded so you can see exactly how it reached the answer.

blueprint.title: What gets built
blueprint.body: The step-by-step assistant, connections to the systems it needs, a searchable index of your reference material, and per-user working state so it can pick up where it left off.
